expectations.text: Explains the difference between Agency ATO and JAB P-ATO
expectations.evidence: Response dedicates a full section comparing Agency ATO and JAB P-ATO, explaining issuer, scope, timeline, and notes that JAB P-ATO is fully suspended under CR26.

expectations.text: Mentions the role of a 3PAO (Third Party Assessment Organization)
expectations.evidence: Response explains 3PAO role in detail: prepares SAP, conducts independent security assessment, produces SAR, and advises engaging one early.

expectations.text: Lists the core FedRAMP documentation: SSP, SAP, SAR, POA&M
expectations.evidence: Response presents the full authorization package structure listing SSP + Appendices A-Q, SAP + Appendices A-D, SAR + Appendices A-F, and POA&M.

expectations.text: Explains FedRAMP Ready designation as a precursor step
expectations.evidence: Response explains FedRAMP Ready and its retirement on July 28, 2026, noting CSPs pursuing it should pivot to FedRAMP 20x — substantively addresses the designation and its current status.

Response includes a dedicated Phase 4 section on ConMon covering monthly requirements (vulnerability scans, POA&M updates, inventory updates) and annual requirements (3PAO assessment, updated SSP, tested IRP/CP).
